revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ' '''天孫族（てんそんぞく）'''とは、[[記紀]]に登場し[[日本神話]]に語り継がれる[[ヤマト王権]]をつくった古代勢力の総…'
revision.text:
'''天孫族（てんそんぞく）'''とは、[[記紀]]に登場し[[日本神話]]に語り継がれる[[ヤマト王権]]をつくった古代勢力の総称。「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では、[[天火明命]]の子孫を『天孫族』と定義している。[[天皇家]]や[[藤原氏]]などの公家が子孫にあたる。
[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため九州地方に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[ヤマト大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とする[[ヤマト王権]]（[[倭国]]）を樹立させた。中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していった。
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]

revision.comment: リダイレクト回避、リンク修正
revision.text:
'''天孫族（てんそんぞく）'''とは、[[記紀]]に登場し[[日本神話]]に語り継がれる[[ヤマト王権]]をつくった古代勢力の総称。「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では、[[天火明命]]の子孫を『天孫族』と定義している。[[天皇家]]や[[藤原氏]]などの公家が子孫にあたる。
[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため九州地方に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[ヤマト大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させた。中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していった。

revision.text:
{{未検証|date=2009年3月}} '''天孫族'''（てんそんぞく）は、[[記紀]]に登場し、[[日本神話]]に語り継がれる、[[ヤマト王権]]をつくったとする古代勢力の総称。「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では、 [[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天道根命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。[[皇室]]、[[隼人]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[土師氏]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。
記紀によると、[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため日向に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させ、中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していったとしている。
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神・国津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]
[[zh:天孫民族]]

revision.comment: ボット: 言語間リンク 1 件を[[Wikipedia:ウィキデータ|ウィキデータ]]上の [[d:Q10939350]] に転記
古典に由緒あり奇異な言葉ではない。
revision.text:
'''天孫族'''（てんそんぞく）は、[[日本神話]]において[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し[[ヤマト王権]]をつくったとする古代勢力の総称。また「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では[[天照大神]]などの子孫を「天孫族」としている。
== 記紀における天孫族 == {{Main|天孫降臨}} 記紀によると、[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため日向に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させ、中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していったとしている。
== 新撰姓氏録における天孫族 == {{Main|新撰姓氏録}} 「[[新撰姓氏録]]」神別では、[[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天道根命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。
[[皇室]]、[[隼人]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[土師氏]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神・国津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[天孫降臨]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]

'''天孫族'''（てんそんぞく）は、[[日本神話]]において出雲系の日本列島創造神話であるいわゆる神世の時代以降に語られる、いわゆる神代の時代の後半における[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し[[ヤマト王権]]をつくったとする古代勢力の総称。また「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では[[天照大神]]などの子孫を「天孫族」としている。
== 記紀における天孫族 == {{Main|天孫降臨}} 記紀によると、[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため日向に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させ、中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していったとしている。
== 新撰姓氏録における天孫族 == {{Main|新撰姓氏録}} 「[[新撰姓氏録]]」神別では、[[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天道根命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。
[[皇室]]、[[隼人]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[土師氏]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神・国津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[天孫降臨]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]

'''天孫族'''（てんそんぞく）は、[[日本神話]]において出雲系の日本列島創造神話であるいわゆる神世の時代以降に語られる、いわゆる神代の時代の後半においてなされた統治形態の完成を語る上で登場した[[天孫降臨|降臨]]や[[ヤマト王権]]をつくったとする古代勢力の総称。また「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では[[天照大神]]などの子孫を「天孫族」としている。
== 記紀における天孫族 == {{Main|天孫降臨}} 記紀によると、[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため日向に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させ、中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していったとしている。
== 新撰姓氏録における天孫族 == {{Main|新撰姓氏録}} 「[[新撰姓氏録]]」神別では、[[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天道根命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。
[[皇室]]、[[隼人]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[土師氏]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神・国津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[天孫降臨]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]

revision.text:
{{神道}} '''天孫族'''（てんそんぞく）は、[[日本神話]]において[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し[[ヤマト王権]]をつくったとする古代勢力の総称。また「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では[[天照大神]]などの子孫を「天孫族」としている。
== 記紀における天孫族 == {{Main|天孫降臨}} 記紀によると、[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため日向に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させ、中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していったとしている。
== 新撰姓氏録における天孫族 == {{Main|新撰姓氏録}} 「[[新撰姓氏録]]」神別では、[[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天道根命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。
[[皇室]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[土師氏]]、[[隼人]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神・国津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[天孫降臨]] * [[アマゾーン]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]

後裔氏族について、嫡流の一派である三上氏が抜けてたため、追記。天照大神の子孫とされる神々の項目に天道根命があるが、天道根命は天照大神の子孫(天孫族)ではない。天神族である。
天照大神の御子神、孫神、曾孫神を併記。

revision.text:
'''天孫族'''（てんそんぞく）は、[[日本神話]]において[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し[[ヤマト王権]]をつくったとする古代勢力の総称。また「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では[[天照大神]]などの子孫を[[神別]]の「天孫」としている。
== 系統と分布 == 天孫族は古代[[中国]]の[[殷]]・[[周]]・[[秦]]・[[羌族]]などの[[東夷]]系種族に通じる種族とする説がある。また[[箕子朝鮮]]の王族ないし[[扶余]]王族、[[高句麗]]や[[百済]]・[[新羅]]、あるいは[[渤海]]を建国した部族と同族の流れを汲むとされる。なお[[北狄]]ともかなりの混血があったためか、習俗で似通ったものがあるとされる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「上古史の流れの概観試論」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref>。
=== 東夷系種族説 === 中国東北地方の[[遼西]]地方周辺を原住地とし、[[朝鮮半島]]を経由して南部の[[弁辰]]を根拠地として、紀元一世紀代前半頃に[[日本列島]]に到来した種族とされる。[[北九州]]の松浦半島に上陸した後は、[[松浦川]]に沿って奥地に溯り、天山南方の[[佐賀平野]]を西から東に進んで、[[筑後川]]の中・下流域、水縄山地（身納山脈）、特に[[高良山]]の北麓から西麓の辺り、[[筑後国]]の[[御井郡]]・[[山本郡]]を中心とする地域に定着したとされる。この種族は[[鉄器]]文化や鳥[[トーテミズム]]を持ち、[[支石墓]]や後期の朝鮮式[[無文土器]]にも関係したとみられる。また、これが『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に見える[[邪馬台国]]の前身たる部族国家（[[高天原]]）で、このような原始国家を[[2世紀]]初頭前後頃から形成し、2世紀後半には分岐国家の[[伊都国]]から[[神武天皇]]兄弟を輩出した<ref>宝賀寿男「神武天皇の原像」『古樹紀之房間』、2014年。</ref>。神武天皇の子孫は[[大和朝廷]]の基礎を作り上げ、残った一族は[[3世紀]]前半の[[魏]]朝の時代に最盛期をむかえ、女王[[卑弥呼]]などを輩出したが、[[4世紀]]代に[[古墳]]文化を所持し、強大な勢力となった[[景行天皇]]や[[神功皇后]]による九州地方の平定によって滅んだものとされる<ref>宝賀寿男「上古史の流れの概観試論」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。</ref>。
== 特徴 == [[製鉄|鉄鍛冶]]・[[製塩]]や[[土器]]・[[銅鏡]]・[[玉]]・[[銅剣]]・鉄剣・[[弓矢]]・[[衣類]]の製作を行った種族。[[熊]]、[[猪]]、[[鳥]]（とくに[[白鳥]]・[[鷹]]・[[鷲]]）といった[[トーテム]]があり、[[太陽信仰]]、[[磐座信仰|巨石]]・[[石神]]信仰、[[製鉄]]・[[風神]]信仰、温泉神、医薬神、[[妙見菩薩|妙見信仰]]を持つ。関係が深い植物には[[粟]]・[[麻]]があり、[[五十猛神]]（[[須佐之男命]]、[[八幡神]]、[[熊野大神]]、角凝魂命）・[[高御産巣日神]]・[[天照大御神]]（天活玉神（伊久魂神））・[[天津彦根命]]（[[天若日子]]）・[[天目一箇命]]（[[天太玉命]]、[[天津麻羅]]、[[経津主神]]）・[[天日鷲神]]（[[陶津耳命]]、[[少名毘古那神]]）などを祖神として奉斎する他、母系神として[[豊宇気毘売神]]（[[菊理姫神]]、[[宇迦之御魂神]]、[[保食神]]）も祀る<ref>宝賀寿男「上古史の流れの概観試論」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男「伊勢津彦と建御名方命との関係」への返信、『古樹紀之房間、掲示板・応答板』、2006年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑭ 蘇我氏 権勢を誇った謎多き古代大族』青垣出版、2019年。</ref>。
== 記紀における天孫族 == {{Main|天孫降臨}} 記紀によると[[国譲り]]の後、[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため日向に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させ、中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していったとしている。
== 新撰姓氏録における天孫族 == {{Main|新撰姓氏録}} 「[[新撰姓氏録]]」神別では、[[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天道根命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。
[[皇室]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[土師氏]]、[[隼人]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。しかし尾張氏はその祭祀形態から[[海神族]]、隼人は習俗から[[山祇族]]ともされる。
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神・国津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[天孫降臨]] * [[天孫氏]] * [[出雲族]]
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{日本の民族}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]

方針[[WP:CITEHOW]]に基づく記述希望。サイト「古樹紀之房間」の多くは過去に書籍・雑誌等印刷物で公刊された[[宝賀寿男]]氏論考の書き写し。原論文の出版年ではなくWebへのUp年が書かれてる場合があるなど不正確。出版年、出版社、ページ数を希望。できれば[[ISSN]]・[[ISBN]]も。また電子掲示板返信文の掲載は[[WP:BLOGS]]抵触の可能性。
/* 系統と分布 */総論を纏めたサイトであるため、ページ番号を求めるのは疑問。

[[中国]]の[[殷]]・[[周]]・[[秦]]を建国した民族は漢族つまり中国人であって、日本人じゃない。
/* 東夷系種族説 */ 『古樹紀之房間』によると[[遼東]]地方である。
/* 東夷系種族説 */
[[Special:Contributions/ABCEdit|ABCEdit]] ([[User talk:ABCEdit|会話]]) による ID:83372837 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]]
IP利用者による出典に基づかない改変を差し戻し
殷族は漢字を使っていて言語は中国語に近い、語族が日本人と全く違う。殷族の故地が内モンゴルというのは根拠がない。ツングース族の故地は満州であって後期中国を支配していた民族。言葉も古代文化も中国語、中国より日本に近い。殷族説は在日台湾人から始まった根拠のない話だという意見もある。

revision.text:
'''天孫族'''（てんそんぞく）は、[[日本神話]]において[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し[[ヤマト王権]]をつくったとする古代勢力の総称。また「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では[[天照大神]]などの子孫を[[神別]]の「天孫」としている。
== 系統と分布 == 天孫族は古代[[朝鮮半島]]北部の[[高句麗]]王族ないし[[夫余|扶余]]系民族に通じる種族とする説がある{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}が、学会では支持されていない。また高句麗や[[百済]]・[[新羅]]、あるいは[[渤海 (国)|渤海]]を建国した部族とも親近関係にあったとされる{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。なお前述の扶余ともかなりの混血があったためか、習俗で似通ったものがあるとされる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/joukosi-nagare.htm 上古史の流れの概観試論]」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref>。
=== 宝賀寿男が提唱した濊貊系種族説 === 宝賀寿男は、現在の朝鮮半島北部の地方を故地とする扶余族などと同系の種族で{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}、朝鮮半島南部から東へ、対馬島を根拠地として、紀元[[1世紀]]前半頃に[[日本列島]]に到来した種族であるとする説を提唱した<ref name = 宝賀2018/>。彼の説によれば、[[北九州]]の松浦半島に上陸した後は、[[松浦川]]に沿って奥地に溯り、天山南方の[[佐賀平野]]を西から東に進んで、[[筑後川]]の中・下流域、水縄山地（身納山脈）、特に[[高良山]]の北麓から西麓の辺り、[[筑後国]]の[[御井郡]]・[[山本郡 (福岡県)|山本郡]]を中心とする地域に定着したとされる。この種族は[[青銅器文化]]や鳥[[トーテミズム]]を持ち{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}、[[支石墓]]や後期の朝鮮式[[無文土器]]にも関係したとみられる。また、これが『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に見える[[邪馬台国]]の前身たる部族国家（[[高天原]]）で、このような原始国家を[[2世紀]]初頭前後頃から形成し、2世紀後半には分岐国家の[[伊都国]]から[[神武天皇]]兄弟を輩出したとする説も同時に提唱されている<ref>宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/jinmuto1.htm 神武天皇の原像]」『古樹紀之房間』、2014年。</ref>。彼の説によれば、神武天皇の子孫は[[大和朝廷]]の基礎を作り上げ、残った一族は[[3世紀]]前半に女王[[卑弥呼]]などを輩出した{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}が、[[4世紀]]代に[[古墳]]文化を所持し、強大な勢力となった[[景行天皇]]や[[神功皇后]]による九州地方の平定によって滅んだものとされる<ref>宝賀寿男「上古史の流れの概観試論」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref><ref name = 宝賀2018>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。</ref>。
== 特徴 == 宝賀寿男は、「天孫族」を以下のように定義付けた。
[[青銅器]]・[[製塩]]や[[土器]]・[[銅鏡]]・[[玉]]・[[銅剣]]・[[弓矢]]・[[衣類]]の製作を行った種族。[[熊]]、[[猪]]、[[鳥]]（とくに[[白鳥]]・[[鷹]]・[[鷲]]）といった[[トーテム]]があり{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}、[[太陽信仰]]、[[磐座信仰|巨石]]・[[石神]]信仰、[[青銅器]]・[[風神]]信仰、温泉神、医薬神、[[妙見菩薩|妙見信仰]]を持つ。関係が深い植物には[[粟]]・[[麻]]があり、[[五十猛神]]（[[須佐之男命]]、[[八幡神]]、[[熊野大神]]、角凝魂命）・[[高御産巣日神]]・[[天照大御神]]（[[天活玉命]]（伊久魂神））・[[天津彦根命]]（[[天若日子]]）・[[天目一箇命]]（[[天太玉命]]、[[天津麻羅]]、[[経津主神]]）・[[天日鷲神]]（[[陶津耳命]]、[[少名毘古那神]]）などを祖神として奉斎する他、母系神として[[豊宇気毘売神]]（[[菊理姫神]]、[[宇迦之御魂神]]、[[保食神]]）も祀る<ref>宝賀寿男「上古史の流れの概観試論」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男「伊勢津彦と建御名方命との関係」への返信、『古樹紀之房間、掲示板・応答板』、2006年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑭ 蘇我氏 権勢を誇った謎多き古代大族』青垣出版、2019年。</ref>。
== 記紀における天孫族 == {{Main|天孫降臨}} 記紀によると[[国譲り]]の後、[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため日向に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させ、中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していったとしている。
== 新撰姓氏録における天孫族 == {{Main|新撰姓氏録}} 「[[新撰姓氏録]]」神別では、[[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。
[[皇室]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[三上氏]]、[[物部氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[山背国造]]、[[凡河内国造|広峯氏]]、[[土師氏]]、[[隼人]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。しかし尾張氏の系図は系譜仮冒であり、またその祭祀形態から[[海神族]]、隼人は習俗から[[縄文人|山祇族]]ともされる。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神・国津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[天孫降臨]] * [[天孫氏]] * [[海神族]] - 天孫族と対をなす種族。[[弥生人]]の一種。 * [[出雲族]] - 出雲神話由縁の種族の総称で、海神族と天孫族が混入する。
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{日本の民族}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

'''天孫族'''（てんそんぞく）は、[[日本神話]]において[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し[[ヤマト王権]]をつくったとする古代勢力の総称。また「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では[[天照大神]]などの子孫を[[神別]]の「天孫」としている。
== 系統と分布 == 天孫族は古代[[朝鮮半島]]北部の[[高句麗]]王族ないし[[夫余|扶余]]系民族に通じる種族とする説がある{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}が、学会では支持されていない。また高句麗や[[百済]]・[[新羅]]、あるいは[[渤海 (国)|渤海]]を建国した部族とも親近関係にあったとされる{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。なお前述の扶余ともかなりの混血があったためか、習俗で似通ったものがあるとされる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/joukosi-nagare.htm 上古史の流れの概観試論]」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref>。
=== 宝賀寿男が提唱した濊貊系種族説 === 宝賀寿男は、現在の朝鮮半島北部の地方を故地とする扶余族などと同系の種族で{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}、朝鮮半島南部から東へ、対馬島を根拠地として、紀元[[1世紀]]前半頃に[[日本列島]]に到来した種族であるとする説を提唱した<ref name = 宝賀2018/>。彼の説によれば、[[北九州]]の松浦半島に上陸した後は、[[松浦川]]に沿って奥地に溯り、天山南方の[[佐賀平野]]を西から東に進んで、[[筑後川]]の中・下流域、水縄山地（身納山脈）、特に[[高良山]]の北麓から西麓の辺り、[[筑後国]]の[[御井郡]]・[[山本郡 (福岡県)|山本郡]]を中心とする地域に定着したとされる。この種族は[[青銅器文化]]や鳥[[トーテミズム]]を持ち{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}、[[支石墓]]や後期の朝鮮式[[無文土器]]にも関係したとみられる。また、これが『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に見える[[邪馬台国]]の前身たる部族国家（[[高天原]]）で、このような原始国家を[[2世紀]]初頭前後頃から形成し、2世紀後半には分岐国家の[[伊都国]]から[[神武天皇]]兄弟を輩出したとする説も同時に提唱されている<ref>宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/jinmuto1.htm 神武天皇の原像]」『古樹紀之房間』、2014年。</ref>。彼の説によれば、神武天皇の子孫は[[大和朝廷]]の基礎を作り上げ、残った一族は[[3世紀]]前半に女王[[卑弥呼]]などを輩出した{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}が、[[4世紀]]代に[[古墳]]文化を所持し、強大な勢力となった[[景行天皇]]や[[神功皇后]]による九州地方の平定によって滅んだものとされる<ref>宝賀寿男「上古史の流れの概観試論」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref><ref name = 宝賀2018>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。</ref>。
== 特徴 == 宝賀寿男は、「天孫族」を以下のように定義付けた。
[[青銅器]]・[[製塩]]や[[土器]]・[[銅鏡]]・[[玉]]・[[銅剣]]・[[弓矢]]・[[衣類]]の製作を行った種族。[[熊]]、[[猪]]、[[鳥]]（とくに[[白鳥]]・[[鷹]]・[[鷲]]）といった[[トーテム]]があり{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}、[[太陽信仰]]、[[磐座信仰|巨石]]・[[石神]]信仰、[[青銅器]]・[[風神]]信仰、温泉神、医薬神、[[妙見菩薩|妙見信仰]]を持つ。関係が深い植物には[[粟]]・[[麻]]があり、[[五十猛神]]（[[須佐之男命]]、[[八幡神]]、[[熊野大神]]、角凝魂命）・[[高御産巣日神]]・[[天照大御神]]（[[天活玉命]]（伊久魂神））・[[天津彦根命]]（[[天若日子]]）・[[天目一箇命]]（[[天太玉命]]、[[天津麻羅]]、[[経津主神]]）・[[天日鷲神]]などを祖神として奉斎する他、母系神として[[豊宇気毘売神]]、[[菊理姫神]]、[[宇迦之御魂神]]、[[保食神]]）も祀る{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}<ref>宝賀寿男「上古史の流れの概観試論」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男「伊勢津彦と建御名方命との関係」への返信、『古樹紀之房間、掲示板・応答板』、2006年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑭ 蘇我氏 権勢を誇った謎多き古代大族』青垣出版、2019年。</ref>。
== 記紀における天孫族 == {{Main|天孫降臨}} 記紀によると[[国譲り]]の後、[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため日向に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させ、中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していったとしている。
== 新撰姓氏録における天孫族 == {{Main|新撰姓氏録}} 「[[新撰姓氏録]]」神別では、[[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。
[[皇室]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[三上氏]]、[[物部氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[山背国造]]、[[凡河内国造|広峯氏]]、[[土師氏]]、[[隼人]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。しかし尾張氏の系図は系譜仮冒であり、またその祭祀形態から[[海神族]]、隼人は習俗から[[縄文人|山祇族]]ともされる。

== 新撰姓氏録における天孫族 == {{Main|新撰姓氏録}} 「[[新撰姓氏録]]」神別では、[[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。
[[皇室]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[三上氏]]、[[物部氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[山背国造]]、[[凡河内国造|広峯氏]]、[[土師氏]]、[[隼人]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。しかし尾張氏の系図は系譜仮冒であり、またその祭祀形態から[[海神族]]、隼人は習俗から[[縄文人|山祇族]]ともされる{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神・国津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[天孫降臨]] * [[天孫氏]] * [[海神族]] - 天孫族と対をなす種族。[[弥生人]]の一種。 * [[出雲族]] - 出雲神話由縁の種族の総称で、海神族と天孫族が混入する。
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{日本の民族}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]

revision.text:
'''天孫族'''（てんそんぞく）は、宝賀寿男が主張した[[日本神話]]において[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し[[ヤマト王権]]をつくったとする古代勢力の総称。また「[[新撰姓氏録]]」では[[天照大神]]などの子孫を[[神別]]の「天孫」としている。
== 系統と分布 == 天孫族は古代[[朝鮮半島]]北部の[[高句麗]]王族ないし[[夫余|扶余]]系民族に通じる種族とする説がある{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}が、学会では支持されていない。また高句麗や[[百済]]・[[新羅]]、あるいは[[渤海 (国)|渤海]]を建国した部族とも親近関係にあったとされる{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。なお前述の扶余ともかなりの混血があったためか、習俗で似通ったものがあるとされる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/joukosi-nagare.htm 上古史の流れの概観試論]」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref>。
=== 宝賀寿男が提唱した濊貊系種族説 === 宝賀寿男は、現在の朝鮮半島北部の地方を故地とする扶余族などと同系の種族で{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}、朝鮮半島南部から東へ、対馬島を根拠地として、紀元[[1世紀]]前半頃に[[日本列島]]に到来した種族であるとする説を提唱した<ref name = 宝賀2018/>。彼の説によれば、[[北九州]]の松浦半島に上陸した後は、[[松浦川]]に沿って奥地に溯り、天山南方の[[佐賀平野]]を西から東に進んで、[[筑後川]]の中・下流域、水縄山地（身納山脈）、特に[[高良山]]の北麓から西麓の辺り、[[筑後国]]の[[御井郡]]・[[山本郡 (福岡県)|山本郡]]を中心とする地域に定着したとされる。この種族は[[青銅器文化]]や鳥[[トーテミズム]]を持ち{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}、[[支石墓]]や後期の朝鮮式[[無文土器]]にも関係したとみられる。また、これが『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に見える[[邪馬台国]]の前身たる部族国家（[[高天原]]）で、このような原始国家を[[2世紀]]初頭前後頃から形成し、2世紀後半には分岐国家の[[伊都国]]から[[神武天皇]]兄弟を輩出したとする説も同時に提唱されている<ref>宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/kodaisi/jinmuto1.htm 神武天皇の原像]」『古樹紀之房間』、2014年。</ref>。彼の説によれば、神武天皇の子孫は[[大和朝廷]]の基礎を作り上げ、残った一族は[[3世紀]]前半に女王[[卑弥呼]]などを輩出した{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}が、[[4世紀]]代に[[古墳]]文化を所持し、強大な勢力となった[[景行天皇]]や[[神功皇后]]による九州地方の平定によって滅んだものとされる<ref>宝賀寿男「上古史の流れの概観試論」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref><ref name = 宝賀2018>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。</ref>。
== 特徴 == 宝賀寿男は、「天孫族」を以下のように定義付けた。
[[青銅器]]・[[製塩]]や[[土器]]・[[銅鏡]]・[[玉]]・[[銅剣]]・[[弓矢]]・[[衣類]]の製作を行った種族。[[熊]]、[[猪]]、[[鳥]]（とくに[[白鳥]]・[[鷹]]・[[鷲]]）といった[[トーテム]]があり{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}、[[太陽信仰]]、[[磐座信仰|巨石]]・[[石神]]信仰、[[青銅器]]・[[風神]]信仰、温泉神、医薬神、[[妙見菩薩|妙見信仰]]を持つ。関係が深い植物には[[粟]]・[[麻]]があり、[[五十猛神]]（[[須佐之男命]]、[[八幡神]]、[[熊野大神]]、角凝魂命）・[[高御産巣日神]]・[[天照大御神]]（[[天活玉命]]（伊久魂神））・[[天津彦根命]]（[[天若日子]]）・[[天目一箇命]]（[[天太玉命]]、[[天津麻羅]]、[[経津主神]]）・[[天日鷲神]]などを祖神として奉斎する他、母系神として[[豊宇気毘売神]]、[[菊理姫神]]、[[宇迦之御魂神]]、[[保食神]]）も祀る{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}<ref>宝賀寿男「上古史の流れの概観試論」『古樹紀之房間』、2009年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男「伊勢津彦と建御名方命との関係」への返信、『古樹紀之房間、掲示板・応答板』、2006年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑬ 天皇氏族 天孫族の来た道』青垣出版、2018年。</ref><ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族の研究⑭ 蘇我氏 権勢を誇った謎多き古代大族』青垣出版、2019年。</ref>。
== 記紀における天孫族 == {{Main|天孫降臨}} 記紀によると[[国譲り]]の後、[[高天原]]より[[葦原中国平定]]のため日向に[[天孫降臨|降臨]]し、中国地方を経て近畿地方まで[[神武東征|東征]]し西日本各地の豪族を従え[[大王 (ヤマト王権)|大王]]（[[天皇]]）を中心とするヤマト王権（[[倭国]]）を樹立させ、中部・関東地方まで勢力を拡大させ、のちの[[日本]]へと発展していったとしている。
== 新撰姓氏録における天孫族 == {{Main|新撰姓氏録}} 「[[新撰姓氏録]]」神別では、[[天照大神]]の子孫とみなされる神々、[[天穂日命]]、[[天津彦根命]]、[[天火明命]]、[[火須勢理命|火闌降命]]、[[天佐鬼利命]]の子孫を『天孫』と定義している。
[[皇室]]、[[出雲国造|出雲氏]]、[[三上氏]]、[[物部氏]]、[[尾張氏]]、[[山背国造]]、[[凡河内国造|広峯氏]]、[[土師氏]]、[[隼人]]などはその子孫にあたるとしている。しかし尾張氏の系図は系譜仮冒であり、またその祭祀形態から[[海神族]]、隼人は習俗から[[縄文人|山祇族]]ともされる{{なぜ|date=2022年3月}}{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Notelist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天津神・国津神]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[天孫降臨]] * [[天孫氏]] * [[海神族]] - 天孫族と対をなす種族。[[弥生人]]の一種。 * [[出雲族]] - 出雲神話由縁の種族の総称で、海神族と天孫族が混入する。
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{日本の民族}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそんそく}} [[Category:日本神話]]

多くの文章が消されている。

信頼できる情報源ではない [[Special:Contributions/240B:10:24A0:F300:690A:FC0E:83D6:E0FE|240B:10:24A0:F300:690A:FC0E:83D6:E0FE]] ([[User talk:240B:10:24A0:F300:690A:FC0E:83D6:E0FE|会話]]) による ID:94539804 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]]
すでにブロックされたアカウントが、博士号を持たない正当な歴史学者としては認められていない者（宝賀寿男）の著作物を出典にして継続的に編集している。 よってそれ以前の、2018年8月29日 (水) 20:51‎ Saigen Jiro の時点まで差し戻します。
廃止された[[Template:神道]]を除去
proxi userの荒らし。

C. |title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus |url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html |access-date=2023-12-11 |website=www.japanesewiki.com |publisher=National Institute of Information and Communications Te..."

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the Yamato Dynasty, which appears in the Kojiki (Records of Ancient Matters) and the Nihonshoki (The Chronicles of Japan) and which is told in Japanese mythology. The Kujiki defines "Tenson" as the offspring of Ame no Hoakari, a deity in Japanese myths. It mentions that the Imperial Family, the Mononobe clan and an ancient tribe of Hayato descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher=[[National Institute of Information and Communications Technology]]}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara]] to conquer Ashihara no nakakuni (literally, the Central Land of Reed Plains) in the [[Kyūshū|Kyushu]] region. Led by Emperor Jinmu they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to [[Kansai region|Kinki]] region. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the Yamato dynasty (Wakoku) which was ruled by the Great King of Yamato (emperor). The dynasty expanded its power to Chubu and Kanto regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from Amenohoakari, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Tanba Province]] before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from Amenohoakari. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |title=海部氏系図 |trans-title=Amebe shikeizu |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777 |accessdate=August 20, 2020 |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |language=Japanese}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|en}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku}}'' descended from {{ill|Takamagahara|en}} (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy. [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kyōiku kanji]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] }} [[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]]

It mentions that the Imperial Family, the Mononobe clan and an ancient tribe of Hayato descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>

It is from early [[Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from Amenohoakari, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of [[Tanba Province]] before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from Amenohoakari. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|en}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku}}'' descended from {{ill|Takamagahara|en}} (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy. [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kyōiku kanji]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] }} [[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]]

It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara]] to conquer {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en}} (literally, the Central Land of Reed Plains) in the [[Kyūshū|Kyushu]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to [[Kansai region|Kinki]] region. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from Amenohoakari, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from Amenohoakari. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|en}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku}}'' descended from {{ill|Takamagahara|en}} (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy. [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[ja:天孫族]]

The document records 82 generations of descent from Amenohoakari. In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|en}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en:draft|9=天孫民族|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en:draft|9=天孫民族|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족}}'' descended from {{ill|Takamagahara|en}} (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]

In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from Amenohoakari, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from Amenohoakari. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|simple|User: Immanuelle/Amanomichine|ja|6=en:draft}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en:draft|9=天孫民族|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en:draft|9=天孫民族|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족}}'' descended from {{ill|Takamagahara|en}} (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of Amenohoakari, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy. [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}. It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en}} (literally, the Central Land of Reed Plains) in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|simple|User: Immanuelle/Amanomichine|ja|6=en:draft}}, together with the descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}} are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en:draft|9=天孫民族|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|8=en:draft|9=天孫民族|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족}}'' descended from {{ill|Takamagahara|en}} (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|simple|User: Immanuelle/Amanomichine|ja|6=en:draft}}, together with the descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}} are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from {{ill|Takamagahara|en}} (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

{{draft}}{{Promising draft}} {{Expand Japanese}}
{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}. It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en}} (literally, the Central Land of Reed Plains) in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|simple|User: Immanuelle/Amanomichine|ja|6=en:draft}}, together with the descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}} are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from {{ill|Takamagahara|en}} (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]

However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kyōiku kanji]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Draft articles]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] }} [[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]]

{{draft}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{complex}} {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|4=fr}} (literally, the Central Land of Reed Plains) in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

revision.comment: /* 関連項目 */ 「出雲族」の定義を[[出雲族]]のページで修正したところ、それによって天孫族との関連性も薄れたため、関連項目から撤去する。

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kyōiku kanji]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Draft articles]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] }} [[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]]

{{draft}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

{{draft}} {{Expand Japanese}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kyōiku kanji]] [[Category:Draft articles]] [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Draft articles]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Wikipedia drafts]] }} [[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]]

It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Original pages]] }} [[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]]

{{draft}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

{{draft}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
