The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[en:Tenson-zoku]]}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}{{complex}} {{User:Immanuelle/Promising Draft}} {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[en:Tenson-zoku]]}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}} == See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[en:Tenson-zoku]]}}

C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>

== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]

The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.

It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. {{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[en:Tenson-zoku]]}}
{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Japanese ethnic groups}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[ja:天孫]]
[[Category:bump]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]]

It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.

The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]

Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]

The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[en:Tenson-zoku]]}}
{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Japanese ethnic groups}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[ja:天孫]]
[[Category:bump]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Tenson-zoku]]

It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Tenson-zoku|Tenson-zoku]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]][[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|天孫族}} {{complex}}
{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{Interlanguage link|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref><!-- content taken from [[Amenohoakari]] --> It mentions that the [[Imperial House of Japan|Imperial Family]], the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en||fr|Hayato}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]]. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the [[List of emperors of Japan|Yamato dynasty]] ({{ill|Wakoku|en}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi}} ([[Emperor of Japan|emperor]]). The dynasty expanded its power to [[Chūbu region|Chubu]] and [[Kantō region|Kanto]] regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" /> [[uk:Тенсон Зоку]] [[ja:天孫族]] [[zh:天孫民族]] [[fr:Tenson-zoku]] [[ja:天孫族]]
== Genealogy == The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early [[Heian period|Heian period]], it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|}} before it was divided into Tamba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr}}, {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|en|4=fr}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en}} and {{ill|Tamba Province|lt=Tanba|en|}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en||fr|clan Owari}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|lt=Amabe|ja|海部氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref> [[ko:천손족]][[zh:天孫民族]]
However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en}} [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi]]. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|ja|高倉下|en|6=fr}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref> [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]][[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]][[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]][[uk:{{PAGENAME}}]]
{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|ja|天孫降臨}} * {{ill|Tenson dynasty|ja|天孫氏}} * {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|ja|弥生人}}.
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Tribes of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/{{PAGENAME}}]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Tenson-zoku]][[en:Tenson-zoku]]}}
{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Japanese ethnic groups}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[ja:天孫]]
[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:qq 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:Tenson-zoku]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenson-zoku]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Tenson-zoku]]

C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{Ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en}}}}</ref>
The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en||fr}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|user:immanuelle/reed bed}}''}} in the [[Kyūshū|Kyūshū]] region. Led by [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] they advanced east through [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku]] region to the [[Kansai region|Kansai region]].
