{{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}

{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato kingship|qq=}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref> It mentions that the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Family}}, the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|fr|Hayato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|qq=}}}}</ref>

The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|User:Immanuelle/reed bed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} in the [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] region. Led by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} they advanced east through {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku}} region to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}}. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|WD=Q188210|lt=Yamato dynasty}} ({{ill|Wakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakoku|qq=}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}}). The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />
== Genealogy ==
The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} before it was divided into Tanba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=draft}}</ref>

In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of water deities|qq=}} {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>

Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}}

The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here  and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}

The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here  and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Tenson-zoku]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}

{{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}

{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato kingship|qq=}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref> It mentions that the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Family}}, the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|fr|Hayato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|qq=}}}}</ref>

The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|User:Immanuelle/reed bed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} in the [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] region. Led by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} they advanced east through {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku}} region to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}}. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|WD=Q188210|lt=Yamato dynasty}} ({{ill|Wakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakoku|qq=}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}}). The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />
== Genealogy ==
The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} before it was divided into Tanba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=draft}}</ref>

In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of water deities|qq=}} {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>

{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato kingship|qq=}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref> It mentions that the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Family}}, the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|fr|Hayato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|qq=}}}}</ref>

The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|User:Immanuelle/reed bed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} in the [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] region. Led by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} they advanced east through {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku}} region to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}}. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|WD=Q188210|lt=Yamato dynasty}} ({{ill|Wakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakoku|qq=}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}}). The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />
== Genealogy ==
The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} before it was divided into Tanba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=draft}}</ref>

In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tenson-zoku]][[ja:天孫族]][[ko:천손족]][[uk:Тенсон Зоку]][[zh:天孫民族]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tenson-zoku]][[ja:天孫族]][[ko:천손족]][[uk:Тенсон Зоку]][[zh:天孫民族]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tenson-zoku]][[ja:天孫族]][[ko:천손족]][[uk:Тенсон Зоку]][[zh:天孫民族]]





{{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}

{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato kingship|qq=}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref> It mentions that the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Family}}, the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|fr|Hayato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|qq=}}}}</ref>

The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|User:Immanuelle/reed bed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} in the [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] region. Led by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} they advanced east through {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku}} region to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}}. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|WD=Q188210|lt=Yamato dynasty}} ({{ill|Wakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakoku|qq=}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}}). The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />
== Genealogy ==
The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} before it was divided into Tanba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=draft}}</ref>

In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of water deities|qq=}} {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>

{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />

The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tenson-zoku]][[ja:天孫族]][[ko:천손족]][[uk:Тенсон Зоку]][[zh:天孫民族]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tenson-zoku]][[ja:天孫族]][[ko:천손족]][[uk:Тенсон Зоку]][[zh:天孫民族]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tenson-zoku]][[ja:天孫族]][[ko:천손족]][[uk:Тенсон Зоку]][[zh:天孫民族]]





{{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}

{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato kingship|qq=}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref> It mentions that the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Family}}, the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|fr|Hayato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|qq=}}}}</ref>

The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|User:Immanuelle/reed bed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} in the [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] region. Led by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} they advanced east through {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku}} region to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}}. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|WD=Q188210|lt=Yamato dynasty}} ({{ill|Wakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakoku|qq=}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}}). The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />
== Genealogy ==
The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} before it was divided into Tanba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=draft}}</ref>

In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of water deities|qq=}} {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi}}. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>

{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|qq=}}}}</ref>

The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />

The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=draft}}</ref>

The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tenson-zoku]][[ja:天孫族]][[ko:천손족]][[uk:Тенсон Зоку]][[zh:天孫民族]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tenson-zoku]][[ja:天孫族]][[ko:천손족]][[uk:Тенсон Зоку]][[zh:天孫民族]]





{{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}

{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato kingship|qq=}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref> It mentions that the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Family|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|fr|Hayato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|qq=}}}}</ref>

The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|User:Immanuelle/reed bed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} in the [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] region. Led by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} they advanced east through {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku|1=Chūgoku region}} region to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}}. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|WD=Q188210|lt=Yamato dynasty|1=List of emperors of Japan}} ({{ill|Wakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakoku|qq=}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}). The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu|1=Chūbu region}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto|1=Kantō region}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />
== Genealogy ==
The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} before it was divided into Tanba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=draft}}</ref>

In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe|1=Amabe clan}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of water deities|qq=}} {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>

{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

* {{ill|Tenson dynasty|en|Tenson dynasty|WD=Q6146852|lt=Tenson dynasty|1=Tenson dynasty}}
* {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama People|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 海人族}} - A clan opposing the Tenson clan, associated with the {{ill|Yayoi people|en|Yayoi people|WD=Q19921451|lt=Yayoi people|1=Yayoi people}}.

{{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}

{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato kingship|qq=}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref> It mentions that the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Family|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|fr|Hayato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|qq=}}}}</ref>

The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|User:Immanuelle/reed bed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} in the [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] region. Led by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} they advanced east through {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku|1=Chūgoku region}} region to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}}. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|WD=Q188210|lt=Yamato dynasty|1=List of emperors of Japan}} ({{ill|Wakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakoku|qq=}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}). The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu|1=Chūbu region}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto|1=Kantō region}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />
== Genealogy ==
The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} before it was divided into Tanba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=draft}}</ref>

In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe|1=Amabe clan}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of water deities|qq=}} {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Tenson-zoku]][[ja:天孫族]][[ko:천손족]][[uk:Тенсон Зоку]][[zh:天孫民族]]





{{Expand Japanese|天孫族}}

{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|8=en:draft|9=|10=simple|11=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|12=ko|13=천손족|14=uk|15=Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族) is a generic term for ancient people that established the {{ill|Yamato kingship|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato kingship|qq=}}, which appears in the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] and which is told in Japanese mythology. The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'' defines "Tenson" as the offspring of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}<ref name="yu-2023a" /> who it identifies with {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|fr|6=en:draft|8=simple|9=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Shoki Mononobe-uji no Keifu (1) Kokigi no Heya (初期物部氏の系譜 (1) 古樹紀之房間)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2007|location=Japan}}</ref> It mentions that the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial Family|1=Imperial House of Japan}}, the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|4=fr|5=clan Mononobe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} and an ancient tribe of {{ill|Hayato people|lt=Hayato|en|fr|Hayato|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} descended from the Tenson tribe.<ref name="yu-2023a">{{Cite web|last=Yu|first=A. C.|title=Tenson-zoku (Tenson Tribe) - Japanese Wiki Corpus|url=https://www.japanesewiki.com/literature/Tenson-zoku%20(Tenson%20Tribe).html|access-date=2023-12-11|website=www.japanesewiki.com|publisher={{ill|National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Institute of Information and Communications Technology|qq=}}}}</ref>

The Tenson tribe descended from [[Takamagahara|Takamagahara]] to conquer {{nihongo|'''{{ill|Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|en|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|qq=}}'''|葦原の中つ国|''The middle country of {{ill|reed bed|en|lt=reed beds|reed bed|simple|User:Immanuelle/reed bed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reed bed|qq=}}''}} in the [[Kyushu|Kyūshū]] region. Led by {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} they advanced east through {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku|1=Chūgoku region}} region to the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}}. Vanquishing the ruling families across western Japan they established the {{ill|List of emperors of Japan|en|List of emperors of Japan|WD=Q188210|lt=Yamato dynasty|1=List of emperors of Japan}} ({{ill|Wakoku|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakoku|qq=}}) which was ruled by the {{ill|Okimi|lt=Great King of Yamato|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okimi|qq=}} ({{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}). The dynasty expanded its power to {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|WD=Q134638|lt=Chubu|1=Chūbu region}} and {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kanto|1=Kantō region}} regions and developed into Japan as we know it today.<ref name="yu-2023a" />
== Genealogy ==
The {{nihongo|Amabe clan genealogy|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu||draft}} is a famous document housed at Kono Shrine. It is from early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, it is considered the oldest family tree in Japan. The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} before it was divided into Tanba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. It was designated a National Treasure in 1972.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web|title=海部氏系図|trans-title=Amebe shikeizu|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/777|publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}}|language=Japanese|accessdate=August 20, 2020|qq=draft}}</ref>

In ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the descendants of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame no Hohi|en|4=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Amanomichine|ja|天道根命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomichine|qq=}}, together with the descendants of Amenohoakari are referred to as {{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}} (天孫族). The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe|1=Amabe clan}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>

However, it is clear that ''{{nihongo|'''Amabe clan genealogy'''|海部氏系図|Amabe-shi Keizu}},'' which records these four clans as descendants of {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, is a forged document, and that these clans actually descended from the {{ill|List of water deities|lt=sea deity|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of water deities|qq=}} {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}. In addition, Owari clan's genealogy includes the great-grandson of Watatsumi, {{ill|Takakuraji|en|Takakuraji|WD=Q11668757|lt=Takakuraji|1=Takakuraji}}, as their ancestor, and this is considered to be the original genealogy.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Tango no Amabe-shi no Shutsuji to sono ichizoku, Kokigi no Heya (丹後の海部氏の出自とその一族)|last=Hoga|first=Toshio|year=2006|location=Japan}}</ref>

{{Wiktionary|天孫族}}

The clan claims descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}, and served as at the ''[[kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]'' of {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} before it was divided into Tanba and Tango. The document records 82 generations of descent from {{ill|Amenohoakari|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|6=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenohoakari|qq=}}. The ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}'' descended from [[Takamagahara]] (Plain of High {{ill|Heaven|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heaven|qq=}}) to {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Tanba Province|lt=Tanba|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} provinces, and are considered to be the ancestors of {{ill|Owari clan|lt=Owari|en|fr|clan Owari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsumori clan|lt=Tsumori|ja|津守氏|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tsumori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|WD=Q65272445|lt=Amabe|1=Amabe clan}}, and {{ill|Tanba clan|lt=Tanba|ja|丹波氏|1=Tanba clan|2=ja|3=丹波氏|4=ko|5=단바씨|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|en|6=fr|7=Clan Tanba|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba clan|qq=}} clans.<ref>{{Cite book|url=http://worldcat.org/oclc/959773242|title=Shinsen Shōjiroku (新撰姓氏錄)|last=Hanawa|first=Hokiichi|date=1983|publisher=Onkogakkai|location=Japan|oclc=959773242|en}}</ref>
